definition: The arising or originating condition of taking personally within dependent-arising analysis.
notes: Use this record when the origin-side compound is named compactly as upadana-samudaya rather than quoted as a fuller origin-line sentence. Keep the compound aligned with the headword defaults for upadana and samudaya, and do not let it collapse into generic attachment-language.

example_phrases.notes: Compact origin-side compound in the dependent-arising family.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use for the compact named upadana-samudaya compound inside the origin-side dependent-arising family; prefer fuller origin-line wording when the canonical sentence itself is present.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not replace the compound with generic attachment-language or mechanically paraphrase away the appropriative force when the compact doctrinal form already fits.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents origin-side upadana language from drifting into generic attachment-language or away from the repository's taking-personally default.
